steps.title: Contrast the sheet structures
steps.content: The notes say graphite is composed of stacked graphene sheets, while carbon nanotubes are composed of graphene sheets wrapped into a cylindrical shape. The answer must emphasize that structural difference directly.

steps.title: Reject incomplete or reversed facts
steps.content: Choice A mentions the nanotube cylinder but leaves out graphite's stacked sheets. Choice B shifts to carbon allotropes instead of emphasizing the contrast. Choice C reverses the facts by making graphite cylindrical and nanotubes flat. The remaining statement contrasts stacked sheets with a cylinder. The answer is D.
